excerpt: O Messenger of Allah, I lack even a single sheet with which to cover myself... once there was hunger and no food to sustain us. Now there is illness, and no remedy to comfort us.
I swear by Him who sent your father as a prophet in truth: for three days now I have not tasted even a morsel of bread.
Fāṭima, let it suffice you for your honor that you are the leader of the women of Paradise.
source_work: Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī and related hadith collections

excerpt: When he reached the age of understanding, I said to him: My son, I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering you.
Father, do that which you have been commanded.
God willing, you will find me among those who are patient.
Bind my hands and feet with rope, so that if I should struggle in pain, my suffering may not afflict your eyes.
O my Lord! O my Friend! Whose love is greater in my heart—the love of Ishmael, or the love of You?
This was indeed the mighty trial with which We tested Abraham... We redeemed him with a great sacrifice... Thus do We reward those who do good. Surely, he was among Our believing servants.
Say, O people, I am the Messenger of Allah to all of you... We sent you not save as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.
source_location_hint: Sūrat al-Aʿrāf, verse 158 and Sūrat Sabā, verse 28

excerpt: Glory be to Him who carried His servant by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Furthest Mosque, whose precincts We have blessed.
He passed by his mother's grave... He descended from his camel and sat at the head of her tomb... he wept as a small child weeps.
source_work: Sīrat Ibn Hishām and related sīra literature
source_author: Ibn Hishām and classical sīra narrators
source_location_hint: The Prophet's farewell pilgrimage, visit to Āmina's grave at Abwāʾ

excerpt: Every living being must taste death... I am leaving behind a perfect goodness that shall purify all creation.
source_work: Sīrat narrative tradition and prophetic saying
source_location_hint: Death of Āmina, mother of the Prophet

excerpt: Quicken your heart with admonition and illuminate it with wisdom. Strengthen it through asceticism, humble it by remembrance of death.
source_work: Attributed to Imam ʿAlī or Qur'ānic commentary tradition
source_author: Imam ʿAlī or early Islamic wisdom literature

excerpt: Present to him the accounts of those who have gone before, and remind him of what befell the nations of old.
source_work: Qur'ānic wisdom and Islamic exhortative tradition
source_location_hint: General counsel on remembrance of past nations

excerpt: Walk through their dwellings and observe the traces they have left behind.
source_work: Attributed to Imam ʿAlī (Nahj al-Balāgha tradition) or Qur'ānic exegesis
source_location_hint: Spiritual counsel on contemplating past dwellings

excerpt: Do not sell your hereafter for the sake of this world!
source_work: Islamic exhortative tradition and Qur'ānic wisdom

excerpt: Love refused to be hidden... When my passion grows intense, my heart yearns... when I seek nearness to my beloved, he draws near to me.
source_work: Classical Sufi poetry and spiritual narrative

excerpt: It is fear of God that brings me here... It is piety that has driven me from the comfort of my home... In His love, I flee from my own suffering; yet His love holds me in thrall.
source_location_hint: Junaid al-Baghdādī and the girl narrative

excerpt: They circumambulate stones, seeking nearness to You; yet their hearts are harder than the very rocks. If they were sincere in their love, their attributes would fade away, and the attributes of love alone would remain, devoted to Truth through remembrance.
source_work: Classical Sufi spiritual critique and poetry
source_location_hint: Junaid al-Baghdādī narrative—spiritual critique of external devotion

excerpt: This girl surpassed him in the knowledge of Allah (maʿrifat Allāh)... Junaid al-Baghdādī fell to the ground in prostration.
source_location_hint: Junaid al-Baghdādī story—recognition of spiritual rank

excerpt: The trees of Syria recognized him as the seal of the prophets, the final messenger of God... the crown of humanity, the Beloved of Allah, the bearer of Paradise—Muzammil, Muddaththir, Ṭā-Hā, Yāsīn.
source_work: Sīrat tradition and Bahīrā the monk narrative
source_location_hint: Bahīrā the monk's encounter with the young Prophet

excerpt: O Messenger of Allah, my companion has captured me... I have small children at home, and they are hungry. If you would permit me, I could go and nurse them.
source_work: Sīrat narrative tradition and related hadith literature
source_location_hint: The deer narrative—Prophet's compassion and covenants

excerpt: Go to your family.
source_location_hint: The deer narrative—Prophet's release of the doe

excerpt: Truth saves; lies destroy.
